lints.name: security_definer_view
lints.title: Security Definer View
lints.description: Detects views defined with the SECURITY DEFINER property. These views enforce Postgres permissions and row level security policies (RLS) of the view creator, rather than that of the querying user
lints.detail: View \`public.v_integration_run_latest\` is defined with the SECURITY DEFINER property

lints.name: security_definer_view
lints.title: Security Definer View

lints.detail: View \`public.alps_bronze_review_state\` is defined with the SECURITY DEFINER property

lints.name: rls_disabled_in_public
lints.title: RLS Disabled in Public
lints.description: Detects cases where row level security (RLS) has not been enabled on tables in schemas exposed to PostgREST
lints.detail: Table \`public.alps_raw_phoenix_2026_03\` is public, but RLS has not been enabled.

lints.name: extension_in_public
lints.title: Extension in Public
lints.description: Detects extensions installed in the \`public\` schema.
lints.detail: Extension \`pgaudit\` is installed in the public schema. Move it to another schema.

lints.name: extension_in_public
lints.title: Extension in Public
lints.description: Detects extensions installed in the \`public\` schema.
lints.detail: Extension \`pg_trgm\` is installed in the public schema. Move it to another schema.

lints.name: rls_policy_always_true
lints.title: RLS Policy Always True
lints.description: Detects RLS policies that use overly permissive expressions like \`USING (true)\` or \`WITH CHECK (true)\` for UPDATE, DELETE, or INSERT operations. SELECT policies with \`USING (true)\` are intentionally excluded as this pattern is often used deliberately for public read access.
lints.detail: Table `public.alps_manual_processing_queue` has an RLS policy `manual_queue_insert` for `INSERT` that allows unrestricted access (WITH CHECK clause is always true). This effectively bypasses row-level security for authenticated.

lints.name: rls_policy_always_true
lints.title: RLS Policy Always True
lints.description: Detects RLS policies that use overly permissive expressions like \`USING (true)\` or \`WITH CHECK (true)\` for UPDATE, DELETE, or INSERT operations. SELECT policies with \`USING (true)\` are intentionally excluded as this pattern is often used deliberately for public read access.
lints.detail: Table `public.alps_manual_processing_queue` has an RLS policy `manual_queue_update` for `UPDATE` that allows unrestricted access (both USING and WITH CHECK are always true). This effectively bypasses row-level security for authenticated.

lints.name: anon_security_definer_function_executable
lints.title: Public Can Execute SECURITY DEFINER Function
lints.description: Detects `SECURITY DEFINER` functions that are callable without signing in. Revoke `EXECUTE`, switch the function to `SECURITY INVOKER`, or move it out of your exposed API schema if it is not meant to be public.
lints.detail: Function `public.audit_config_changes()` can be executed by the `anon` role as a `SECURITY DEFINER` function via `/rest/v1/rpc/audit_config_changes`. Revoke `EXECUTE` or switch it to `SECURITY INVOKER` if that is not intentional.
